relevance_results.reason: Discusses movie production costs, mentioning that movies are too expensive and take a long time to make, which contributes to cost. It also touches on effects.
Focuses on the rise of online streaming and its popularity, with only a brief mention of past movie ticket costs, not production expenses.
Directly addresses movie budgets, explaining that large budget movies cost millions and that celebrity salaries alone can be over $20M, contributing significantly to overall cost.
Discusses movie revenue in relation to budget, but doesn't explain the factors contributing to high production costs beyond mentioning the budget itself.
Explains that visual effects can cost tens of millions of dollars due to the extensive labor, time, and highly paid specialist personnel required.

key_facts: Making a movie with a lot of effects can cost 10 to 15 million dollars.
It takes three and a half to four years to make a Star Wars film.

key_facts: A decade ago, four people seeing a movie in a theater could cost more than 70 dollars.
